title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. montana
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. montana, also known as the alpine bellflower or Scottish bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family native to mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Campanula rotundifolia subsp. montana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
